question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted timestamp upper bound just before a known balance snapshot to cause `getFirstDayOfMonth(maxTimestamp, -1)` to silently advance `minTimestamp` past the user-requested lower bound, effectively hiding older historical supply records and presenting a reorganized view of supply history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamp range where `minTimestamp` equals `maxTimestamp` at a nanosecond boundary to force `accountBalanceRepository.getSupplyHistory()` to return a single snapshot that misrepresents the historical ordering of supply changes, causing consumers to believe a different supply state existed at that point in time?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user pass a `timestamp` with operator `lte` set to a value just before a large balance event so that `adjustUpperBound()` returns that value minus one, causing the supply history query to exclude that event and present a historically inaccurate supply figure to downstream systems?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Math.max(minTimestamp, optimalLowerBound)` logic in `getSupply()` by providing a `maxTimestamp` near a month boundary so that `getFirstDayOfMonth(maxTimestamp, -1)` produces an `optimalLowerBound` that silently overrides the user-supplied lower bound, causing the returned supply history to omit legitimate historical records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user provide two timestamp parameters where the first uses `gt` and the second uses `lt` with values that differ by exactly one nanosecond, causing `getAdjustedLowerRangeValue()` and `adjustUpperBound()` to produce an empty effective range that returns a `consensusTimestamp` of 0, triggering an `EntityNotFoundException` that masks the true historical supply state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `timestamp` with `eq` operator that `Bound.of()` silently rewrites to `lte`, causing `getSupply()` to query a broader historical range than the user specified, potentially returning a supply figure from a different point in history and misrepresenting the transaction record?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user pass a `timestamp` with no lower bound so that `getAdjustedLowerRangeValue()` returns 0, and then `Math.max(0, optimalLowerBound)` selects the first day of the month before `maxTimestamp`, causing the supply history to silently skip all records before that computed date and reorganize the visible transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `maxTimestamp` at exactly midnight UTC on the first day of a month so that `getFirstDayOfMonth(maxTimestamp, -1)` computes an `optimalLowerBound` equal to the first day of the previous month, causing the supply history window to span exactly one month and exclude records from the current month that would otherwise be visible?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user provide a `timestamp` upper bound of `Long.MAX_VALUE` nanoseconds to cause `adjustUpperBound()` to return `Long.MAX_VALUE`, making `getFirstDayOfMonth(Long.MAX_VALUE, -1)` compute an astronomically far-future date that overrides any user-supplied lower bound and returns an unexpected historical supply snapshot?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `Bound.EMPTY` causes `getSupply()` to call `entityRepository.getSupply()` instead of `accountBalanceRepository.getSupplyHistory()`, by sending a request with no timestamp parameter, to receive a supply figure derived from a different data source than the historical path, creating an inconsistency in the apparent transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `file.id` parameter pointing to an older address book file to cause `getAddressBookFileId()` to return a historical file ID, making `findNetworkNodes()` query a stale address book and present a reorganized view of which nodes participated in consensus at a given time?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user pass `order=DESC` to `getNetworkNodes()` so that `orderDirection` is set to `'DESC'` and passed directly into the native SQL `case when :orderDirection = 'DESC' then abe.node_id end desc` clause, causing the historical node list to appear in reverse order and misrepresenting the sequence in which nodes were registered?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit multiple `node.id` parameters combining `eq` and range operators so that the `HashSet<Long> nodeIds` is populated and then filtered by `Range.closed(lowerBound, upperBound)`, causing certain node IDs to be silently excluded from the result and presenting an incomplete historical view of the network topology?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user set `node.id=gt:0` and `node.id=lt:1` to cause `lowerBound` to be 1 and `upperBound` to be 0 after `getInclusiveValue()` adjustments, triggering the `lowerBound > upperBound` check and throwing `IllegalArgumentException`, which could be used to probe the boundary of valid node ID ranges and infer historical node registration order?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user pass `limit=25` (the `MAX_LIMIT`) combined with a `node.id=gte:0` range to cause `findNetworkNodes()` to return the maximum page of nodes ordered by `start_consensus_timestamp desc` from the latest address book, allowing the attacker to enumerate the full historical node set and reconstruct the consensus committee history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user omit the `file.id` parameter so that `getAddressBookFileId()` defaults to `systemEntity.addressBookFile102().getId()`, and then compare results with an explicit `file.id=101` request to observe differences between address book versions, effectively reconstructing the history of network topology changes without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `node.id=eq:0` parameter so that `nodeIds` contains `{0}` and `Range.closed(0, Long.MAX_VALUE)` includes it, causing `findNetworkNodes()` to query for node ID 0 which may correspond to a genesis or bootstrap node, revealing historical address book entries that expose the original network configuration?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user pass an empty `nodeIds` array (default) with `lowerBound=0` and `upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE` to cause `EMPTY_NODE_ID_ARRAY` to be used in `findNetworkNodes()`, triggering the `coalesce(array_length(:nodeIds, 1), 0) = 0` branch that returns all nodes, allowing full enumeration of the historical address book without any filtering?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getEffectiveLimit()` caps at `MAX_LIMIT=25` but the underlying `findNetworkNodes()` SQL uses `limit :limit` directly, by passing `limit=1` to retrieve only the first node in a given order and then iterating with different `node.id=gt:X` parameters to page through the entire historical node list and reconstruct address book history one entry at a time?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user pass `registered_node_id=gte:0` to `getRegisteredNodes()` so that `resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds()` sets `lowerBound=0` and `upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing `findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()` to return all non-deleted registered nodes, allowing full enumeration of the current and historical node registration state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user combine `registered_node_id=gt:X` and `registered_node_id=lt:X+1` to cause `resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds()` to compute `lowerBound > upperBound` after `getInclusiveValue()` adjustments, triggering `IllegalArgumentException('Invalid range: lower bound exceeds upper bound')`, which leaks information about the valid range of registered node IDs and their historical ordering?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user pass `registered_node_id=eq:X` combined with a second range parameter to trigger the `IllegalArgumentException('The eq operator cannot be combined with other operators')` check in `resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds()`, using the error response to probe which registered node IDs exist and reconstruct the historical sequence of node registrations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user pass `order=DESC` to `getRegisteredNodes()` so that `Sort.by(request.getOrder(), REGISTERED_NODE.REGISTERED_NODE_ID.getName())` sorts results in descending order, causing the pagination links generated by `linkFactory.create()` to present the registered node history in reverse chronological order and mislead consumers about the sequence of node registrations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user pass `type=<valid_type_id>` to `getRegisteredNodes()` to filter nodes by `RegisteredNodeType`, causing `findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()` to return only nodes of that type, allowing the attacker to enumerate the historical registration of specific node categories and reconstruct the timeline of network topology changes by type?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user pass `limit=MAX_LIMIT` with `registered_node_id=gte:0` and `order=ASC` to retrieve the first page of registered nodes, then use the `links.next` cursor from the response to iterate through all pages, reconstructing the complete historical sequence of node registrations without any authentication?
